title: Cisco ISE Restore Audit and Configuration-Drift Detection Post-Restore
dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Administrative and Operational Audit* category — restore codes `51040` (restore initiated), `51041` (restore completed), `51042` (restore failed), `51043` (restore partial), `51044` (post-restore replication mismatch). Fields: `restore_source`, `triggered_by`, `restore_type` (config|operational|standalone). Combined with the ISE config-export feed: an OpenAPI poller dumps the active policy set (`/api/v1/authz-policy`, `/api/v1/identity-store`) every 1h and writes to `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:config_export`.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (51040, 51041, 51042, 51043, 51044))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:config_export")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event values(triggered_by) as admins by restore_type, MessageCode
| where count > 0
description: Audits all ISE restore operations (rare but high-impact events) and detects config drift in the immediate post-restore window — symptoms of an incomplete restore or a configuration roll-back gone wrong.
value: Restoring ISE from backup is a high-risk operation; it overwrites current policies with the policies from the backup snapshot. Any in-flight policy work between the backup time and the restore time is lost. This UC ensures every restore is audited in real time and triggers a config-drift comparison against pre-restore state to surface unexpected loss.
implementation: Confirm *Administrative and Operational Audit* category at INFO. The config-export OpenAPI poller is required for the drift comparison. Schedule the search every 30 min, search window `-4h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0 (any restore event is alert-worthy).
visualization: (1) Audit table: restore events last 365d. (2) Diff panel post-restore: count of authz rules changed, identity stores reconfigured, certs replaced.

references.title: Cisco ISE 3.4 Admin Guide — Backup and Restore

knownFalsePositives: (a) **Lab restore drills** — Periodic restore exercises in lab environments will fire. Tag those nodes with `environment=lab` and exclude. (b) **Disaster-recovery exercise** — Annual DR exercise restores prod backup to DR; suppress within scheduled DR-exercise window.
mitreAttack: N/A (platform lifecycle — ISE restore drill validation, no adversary technique)

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 10.2 audit trails — restore events are auditable system actions.
SOX-ITGC change management — restore is a change requiring audit.
Art.21(2)(c) — restore is part of backup/restore lifecycle.
CP-10 (System Recovery and Reconstitution) — restore evidence.
compliance.controlObjective: Continuity-of-operations evidence for ISE backup-and-restore readiness.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Initiate a restore on a lab ISE. ISE emits 51040 followed by 51041. UC-17.1.48 captures both events.
controlTest.negativeScenario: No restore activity. UC-17.1.48 returns no rows.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Same as UC-17.1.47.
• Config-export poller deployed.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Double-check *Administrative and Operational Audit* INFO. Deploy config-export poller.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 30 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke a lab restore.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Restore Audit":
• 365-day audit log table.
• Drift diff panel post-restore.

Notification: Slack to `#ise-platform` on any restore event.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Restore initiated but no completion event** — Restore in progress; large operational-data restores can take 30–60 min.
• **Drift panel shows huge diff** — Backup was significantly older than current state; coordinate with the change team to reapply lost changes.
• **`triggered_by` empty** — Restore was triggered via CLI; admin user not captured in syslog. Cross-reference with shell login records.
grandmaExplanation: If someone restores ISE from a safety copy — like rolling back to last week's notebook — we record exactly what happened so we know what was kept and what was lost.
